intro: Tetradema asperifolium, also known as the prickly-leaved tetradema, is a shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its small, white flowers and distinctive thorny leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
